content:
**Nicușor Dan Elected Romania’s New President in Historic 2025 Vote**

Bucharest, May 19, 2025 — After a tumultuous election season marked by a court-ordered re-run, Nicușor Dan has emerged as the new President of Romania, securing victory in the decisive runoff held on May 18.

Dan, the former mayor of Bucharest and a prominent civic activist, won with 54.8% of the vote, defeating his opponent in an election closely watched both domestically and by the European Union. The rerun was necessitated after the Constitutional Court annulled the initial November 2024 results, citing procedural irregularities.

In his victory speech at University Square last night, Dan called for unity and reform: “Romania has chosen a new path—one of transparency, integrity, and progress. Together, we will build a future that honors the hopes of every citizen.”

Major contenders including George Simion, Victor Ponta, Elena Lasconi, and Crin Antonescu all conceded in the first round, leaving Dan to face a runoff challenger. Widespread voter turnout and peaceful polling were praised by international observers, who called the process “free, fair, and democratic.”

Outgoing President Klaus Iohannis congratulated Dan, stating, “Romania’s democracy has proven resilient. I wish President-elect Dan every success in leading our country forward.”

Dan is set to be inaugurated in early June, promising to prioritize anti-corruption measures and economic modernization in his first months in office.
